A.text: Two pentagons share a common edge.

B.text: No two hexagons share a common edge.

C.text: Each hexagon is adjacent only to pentagons.

D.text: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.

parsed.answer_value: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.
parsed.rationale: The image shows a truncated icosahedron, which is the structure of a buckyball (C60). In this structure, every pentagonal face is surrounded by hexagonal faces. This means each pentagon shares its edges exclusively with hexagons. Conversely, hexagons can share edges with other hexagons as well as pentagons. Therefore, option D correctly describes the adjacency rule for pentagons in this specific molecular geometry.
